content.problemStatement:
There's an array **A** consisting of **N** **non-zero** integers **A1..N**. A subarray of **A** is called alternating if any two adjacent elements in it have different signs (i.e. one of them should be negative and the other should be positive).


For each **x** from **1** to **N**, compute the length of the longest alternating subarray that starts at **x** - that is, a subarray **Ax..y** for the maximum possible **y ≥ x**. The length of such a subarray is **y-x+1**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (the length of the array). The second line contains N space-separated non-zero integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output one line with N space-separated integers. The i-th integer is the length of the longest subarray starting at position i that alternates in sign.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test: no adjacent opposite signs, so each answer is 1. Second test: signs alternate everywhere, giving decreasing lengths from left to right. Third test: only positions 3‑5 form a length‑3 alternating segment.
First case same as before. Second case the whole array alternates, so lengths are N, N‑1, …, 1.
All numbers are positive, therefore no two consecutive elements have opposite signs.

solution.approach: Traverse the array from right to left. Let ans[i] be the answer for position i (0‑based). The last element always forms a subarray of length 1, so ans[N‑1] = 1. For i from N‑2 down to 0, if A[i] and A[i+1] have opposite signs (their product is negative) then ans[i] = ans[i+1] + 1, otherwise ans[i] = 1. This works because a longest alternating subarray starting at i either stops at i (if the next element has the same sign) or extends the longest alternating subarray starting at i+1 by one element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the answer array (can be O(1) extra if printed on the fly).
